snippet.title: A Strong Culture For A Carbon Neutral Future | With Dave Burritt
snippet.description:
On this week’s episode of The Leader Show on Newsweek, we are joined by Dave Burritt, the CEO of US Steel, who delves into the company's transformation journey, focusing on the balance between purpose, profit, and planet.

In this episode, our host Louis Carter has a thought-provoking discussion with David Burritt; shedding light on the challenges and opportunities in the steel industry. They discuss the importance of safety in the workplace, the role of sustainability, and how US Steel has internalized "Made in America" as a key business principle.

David further suggests that an innovative and collaborative environment, along with a culture of trust and respect can not only help boost productivity but also helps make a company agile with consistent improvement.

You can read the episode summary here: https://mostlovedworkplace.com/podcast/a-strong-culture-for-a-carbon-neutral-future/

Let us know your thoughts on this insightful interview in the comments!
tags: A strong culture for a carbon neutral future

snippet.title: No.1 Habit & Procrastination Expert: We've Got ADHD Wrong! Break Any Habit & Never Be Distracted!
snippet.description:
In this new episode Steven sits down with the American behavioural design expert, Nir Eyal.

Topics:
00:00 Intro
02:02 Being indistractable
10:21 4 steps to avoid distractions
17:37 10-minute rule to beat procrastination
27:40 Prevent distraction & improve sex life
34:56 How to become disciplined & have strong willpower
47:33 The power of time allocation
53:33 Stop burnout
01:00:27 Stop blaming social media
01:10:17 Overcoming our traumas and improving confidence
01:20:39 Why is there a rise in ADHD?
01:37:31 Last guest’s question

snippet.title: Marek PASIECZNY | The Elements: Triple Concerto | feat. Bion Tsang, Thomas Burritt & Joe Williams
snippet.description:
stream / download https://li.sten.to/TheElements

The Elements: Triple Concerto for Guitar, Cello, Percussion and Guitar Orchestra (2022)
composed and orchestrated by Marek Pasieczny

conducted by Joe Williams w/ HOME Guitar Orchestra
soloists: Marek Pasieczny (guitar★) Bion Tsang (cello), Thomas Burritt (percussion★★)

I. Darkness 0:00
II. Opening 0:56
III. Earth’s Theme 2:09
IV. Solo Cadenza 6:31
V. Interlude to Wind, Air 7:35
VI. Wind’s Theme 10:32
VII. Interlude to Water, Storm 16:11
VIII. Water’s Theme 18:20
IX. Interlude to Void, Souls 24:20
X. Void’s Theme 25:13
XI. Postlude to Void, Ghosts 28:05
XII. Double Cadenza 30:21
XIII. Fire’s Theme 31:50
XIV. Triple Cadenza, Fugue 34:22
XV. Brightness, Finale 35:47

bonus: Finale (encore) 37:47

◾  world premiere at the Aisd Performing Arts Center, Austin, TX, USA, (February 18th, 2023)

◾ ABOUT THE PIECE◾ 

◾ GENESIS 
This composition was commissioned by and dedicated to ACG (Austin Classical Guitar https://austinclassicalguitar.org/acg-originals-home-program/, for which I was appointed Artist in Residence for the year 2022-23. I was asked to create a piece that largely reflects the theme of ACG's 2022-23 artistic year, which was 'HOME.' I also knew that this composition had to be written for five separate groups of guitarists. Each group could independently prepare their assigned portion of the larger whole, but, more importantly, they were to perform that section and treat it as a finished and stand-alone piece.

These two factors ('HOME' and the five independent guitar groups) directed my inspiration towards the philosophy of go-dai, which I had used previously when composing the 'Go-Dai Concerto'  https://youtu.be/HpiGZgvakFg?si=-UQ6P0CrlbSxDGkq

◾ 'GO-DAI' 
The philosophy of Go-Dai is a Japanese concept of five elements known as godai, or the "five great": air/wind (Japanese: 風, fū or kaze), fire (Japanese: 火, ka or hi), water (Japanese: 水, sui or mizu), earth (Japanese: 地, chi or tsuchi), and void/sky (Japanese: 空, kū or sora).

◾ 'HOME' 
For me, "HOME" broadly refers to our planet as our shared home, regardless of where we live. Therefore, my initial intention was to reference weather conditions through the elements of the Go-Dai philosophy.

◾ ELEMENTS 
The first step in the composition process was to develop the main themes for each of the five Go-Dai elements. Each of these themes, along with their harmonization, development, and adaptation to specific guitar groups, was shaped by the character of each element. This way, the music not only conveyed the essence of each element but also visually and suggestively impacted the listener. The choice of expressive techniques, compositional solutions, and structural elements such as meter, harmony, rhythm, tempo, and structure were closely tied to each of the five elements during the composition process.

◾ FORM / ORCHESTRATION 
Despite there being five independent compositions (elements), I strongly wanted to avoid a suite-like form with clear and distinct parts. My goal was to integrate all five elements while maintaining their individualism, much like in nature. During the creation of THE ELEMENTS, I was given the choice to write a solo part for the guitar. This inspired me to consider additional solo parts on various instruments. First, I chose the cello as an instrument that blended well with the guitar's sound. The second addition was a solo part for a percussionist who was to handle over twenty percussion instruments, which I felt naturally relates to the fundamental elements of nature.

The piece begins with abstract, low, and primal sounds symbolizing darkness. Within the structure of the triple concerto, THE ELEMENTS features three main solo cadenzas: solo (timpani), duet (guitar and cello), and trio - fugue (guitar, cello, and marimba). The entire composition is interspersed with abstract moments that serve as links between the various elements. These abstract segments contrast with meticulously developed motifs for each of the elements.

The composition concludes with a bright, cheerful finale, symbolizing transformation: from emptiness and darkness to brightness, fullness, and hope.

Marek Pasieczny
(Leeds, UK 13 Oct. 2023)


The HOME Guitar Orchestra:

◾ Bedichek Middle School
◾ Akins High School 
◾ ACG Youth Orchestra 
◾ UT Austin Ensemble 
◾ UT San Antonio Ensemble 
◾ UT Rio Grande Valley 
◾ ACG Chamber Ensemble 

all info: https://austinclassicalguitar.org/acg-originals-home-program/

★ Marek Pasieczny plays on 'Mehliana' op. 377 by Philip Woodfield https://woodfieldguitars.com
strings: D'Addario EJ46FF  https://www.daddario.com/artists/guitar/marek-pasieczny/

★★ Percussion:
Pitched:
Crotales (w/ bow, triangle beater)
Glockenspiel
Marimba
Thai Gongs (e, f, a flat)
Timpani (32'' 29'' and 26'')
Vibraphone

Non-Pitched:
Castanets
Claves
Gong (w/drum brushes, w/ bow)
Rainstick
Sus. Cymbal
Temple bowl
Thunder Tube
Tibetan Tingsha
Triangle (small)
Wind Chimes (bamboo & metal)

snippet.title: “PERCUZZ!!” on RTHK “The Works” - M.A.R.B.L.E.S. Percussion x Justin Siu & Samuel Chan
snippet.description:
“PERCUZZ” - “percuss” x “jazz” – 
percussion music in a jazz style. Or is it jazz in a percussive style? This production is one of works of Bach and Philip Glass, but also tunes by Miles Davis and Chick Corea, in a band formed from both classical and jazz musicians – talk about fusion. We hope that you’ll find this Saturday evening to be truly spectacular!

日期：2023年9月2日（星期六） 
時間：8pm
地點：牛池灣文娛中心劇院 
票價：$240 
（全日制學生及長者可享有75折）
門票由即日起於城市售票網發售
(link in bio!!)
https://www.urbtix.hk/event-detail/10467/

Date:    11th Nov, 2023 (Sat)
Time:    8pm
Venue:  Theatre, Ngau Chi Wan Civic Centre 
Ticket Available NOW on URBTIX ($240)
(Full time students and senior citizens aged 60 or above : 25% off) 


樂手 Performers : 

M.A.R.B.L.E.S. Percussion : 
阮曼霖 Lauren Yuen - Vibraphone 
陳俊燊 Alvin Chan - Vibraphone 
楊清嵐 Michael Yeung - Marimba 
何子輝 Sunny Ho - Marimba 

Special guests :
陳衍光 Samuel Chan - Drums 
蕭偉中 Justin Siu - Bass
